pattern: A single swap can only fix two positions, so the strings must be equal lengths with exactly two mismatches that are mirror images, or be identical with a repeated letter to swap harmlessly.
hints: A swap touches exactly two positions, so think about how many places the two strings differ and what those places must look like.
If the strings differ, they must differ in exactly two spots, and the two mismatched pairs must be each other's mirror so swapping resolves both.
When the strings are already equal, a valid swap exists only if some letter appears at least twice; otherwise require exactly two differing indices i, j with s[i]==goal[j] and s[j]==goal[i].

edge_inputs.description: single adjacent swap fixes the string
identical strings with all-distinct letters cannot be swapped to stay equal
identical strings with a repeated letter allow a harmless swap
exactly one differing position is impossible with a single swap
two differing positions but not mirror images
more than two differences cannot be fixed by one swap
single character identical strings have no two indices to swap
swap deep inside a longer matching string

stress.description: random base string, then half the time keep it equal and half the time apply a real swap, so both true and false outcomes appear often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    mode = rng.random()
    if mode < 0.5:
        goal = s
    else:
        lst = list(s)
        i = rng.randrange(n)
        j = rng.randrange(n)
        lst[i], lst[j] = lst[j], lst[i]
        goal = ''.join(lst)
        if rng.random() < 0.2:
            k = rng.randrange(n)
            goal = goal[:k] + chr((ord(goal[k]) - 97 + 1) % 26 + 97) + goal[k+1:]
    return [s, goal]
stress.note: narrow alphabet maximises repeated-letter and mirror-pair cases
